definition: Aging populations create a structural bid for fixed-income assets via pension funds and insurers (~$35–40T globally), compressing credit spreads, but this compression may mask rather than reflect benign credit fundamentals.
evidence: Pension funds and insurers hold ~$35–40T in fixed income globally
ALM-driven demand increases structurally with aging liabilities
Compressed spreads in an aging economy may mask credit deterioration
open_questions: How much demographic spread compression is already priced
Whether the demographic bid provides a floor in downturns or merely delays repricing
